# Brief 01 — B2B SaaS startup, mild asymmetry

**Outcome:** Win 50 paying customers in the next 6 months for our developer-focused error monitoring tool.

**Audience:** Backend engineers at Series A-B startups (10-50 engineers), currently using a well-known incumbent tool that costs ~$400/mo per seat.

**Sector:** B2B SaaS, developer tools.

**Budget:** $8k/month marketing, $3k/month of that is paid. Team: one founder-engineer, one part-time marketer (15 hrs/week).

**Competition:** The incumbent spends roughly $40M/year on marketing — conference sponsorships, paid search, outbound SDRs. Ratio roughly 1:400 on paid spend. There are also 3 smaller competitors in our price tier.

**Existing channels:** Founder has ~3k Twitter followers in the dev community, posts weekly. Company blog with 4 posts total. No community, no newsletter, no podcast presence.

**Bottleneck:** Developers trust other developers, not vendors. Cold paid ads don't work for this buyer. Trials convert at 18% which is decent but we can't get enough trials.

**Time window:** 6 months to hit the target, then Series A raise.

**Capacity:** Founder can commit 6 hrs/week to marketing, marketer 15 hrs/week. Total 21 hrs/week.
